findings.evidence: `rg "bernsteinKernel.*chafaiRescaling|chafaiRescaled.*bernsteinKernel|ae.*chafaiMeasure" code/TauCeti` finds no bridge; the only exposed facts are `chafaiRescaling_coe_of_pos` at lines 312-315 and the generic `chafaiRescaled_integral` at lines 338-343.
findings.fix: Add characteristic interaction lemmas, e.g. `bernsteinKernel_chafaiRescaling` for `2 ≤ n`, `0 < t`, and an integral specialization for `bernsteinKernelBoundedContinuous` against `chafaiRescaled f n` using `chafaiMeasure` on `(0,∞)`.
findings.issue: `chafaiRescaled_integral` only gives a generic pullback along `chafaiRescaling`; there is no public lemma rewriting the intended test integral with `bernsteinKernel` after the rescaling, so consumers cannot use the Bernstein approximation API without unfolding `bernsteinKernel`/`chafaiRescaling` and deriving `t > 0` a.e. from `chafaiMeasure_compl_Ioi`.

summary: The new public measure/kernel objects are exposed without the characteristic lemma that connects the rescaled measure to the Bernstein kernel, leaving the roadmap consumer to unfold both definitions and redo the a.e.-positivity bookkeeping.
